name: George Penston
text: Izzy had all the best sketches

name: George Penston
text: Izzy always had the best sketches.

name: James Torres
text: I will never forget the time Izzy played Jafar in our school production. They absolutely owned that role — the voice, the mannerisms, everything. The whole audience was cracking up. Izzy had this gift for making every character bigger than life.

name: Marcus Webb
text: Izzy was the best DM I ever played with. They built entire worlds from scratch — maps, lore, NPCs with actual backstories. Our Tuesday night campaigns were the thing I looked forward to every week. They always found a way to surprise us, even when we thought we had the plot figured out.

name: Priya Sharma
text: Working on Interfectorem with Izzy was one of the highlights of my teenage years. They were the creative engine of our whole team — always sketching characters in the margins of their notebook, always pushing us to make the story darker and weirder. When we won at Demo Day, Izzy just looked at all of us and said "obviously." Classic.

name: Jordan Kim
text: I met Izzy at FanimeCon years ago. They were in the most elaborate Jafar cosplay I'd ever seen and I had to ask how they made it. We ended up talking for three hours. Every year after that, finding Izzy was the first thing I looked forward to when I walked through the doors.

name: Ms. Reyes
text: Izzy had a way of asking questions that made the whole class think differently. Not to show off — they were genuinely curious. I remember one afternoon they stayed after class just to keep talking about a documentary we'd watched. That kind of intellectual hunger is rare, and it was entirely Izzy's own.

name: Theo Nakamura
text: Izzy and I ended up as partners for an anthropology project on ritual and community. They brought in this whole angle about fandom and cosplay as modern ritual that completely changed the direction of our paper. Got us an A. I don't think I would have gotten there without them.

name: Canine Companions volunteer
text: I was part of the team at the graduation ceremony when Izzy was matched with Maeve. The moment those two met, Maeve just settled right in and put her head on Izzy's lap like she'd been waiting for exactly that person. It's one of the most beautiful things I've seen in this work.

name: Devon Okafor
text: Growing up next door to the Penstons, I spent more afternoons at their house than my own. Izzy was the one who got me into reading — they just kept handing me books and refusing to take them back until I'd finished. I still have a copy of Coraline that technically belongs to them.

name: Lily Tran
text: Izzy volunteered at the shelter the same days I did for about a year. They had this way with the shy cats — the ones that hid at the back of their kennels and wouldn't come out for anyone. Izzy would just sit quietly near them and wait. Every single time, the cat came out.

name: Nadia Petrov
text: Izzy and I met in a Stephen King forum and talked almost every week for three years before I ever heard their voice. They had the sharpest takes on the books — not just what worked, but why. Losing them feels like losing a person I knew deeply, even across all those miles.

name: Mr. Yamamoto
text: I taught a lot of students over the years, but Izzy was one I never forgot. Five years old and already asking questions I had to think hard about. I heard they went on to do remarkable things. That didn't surprise me one bit.

name: Bex Holloway
text: We did Up the Down Staircase together and Izzy stole every scene they were in. Off stage they were just as funny — always the person who could break the tension in tech week when everyone was losing their minds. That production is one of my favorite memories from high school and a lot of that is because of them.

name: Sam Delgado
text: I've known the Penstons for over a decade. What I'll always remember about Izzy is how they made you feel genuinely listened to. You'd say something offhand and weeks later they'd bring it back up — some detail they'd held onto. That's a rare quality. They made people feel like they mattered.

name: Clara Nguyen
text: Izzy joined our club just over a year ago and immediately became everyone's favorite. They had this infectious excitement about every single find — it didn't matter if it was a common piece of quartz. They'd hold it up like it was treasure. That enthusiasm reminded all of us why we started doing this.
